title: DirectoryList with 10K users can exceed 1 MiB client WS max_message_size
description: transport.rs sets WebSocketConfig::default().max_message_size(Some(1024 * 1024)) (1 MiB). DirectoryListParams caps at 10_000 entries. Each UserInfo has pub_id (64 hex chars), optional nickname (≤32 chars), and sequence (u64). At 10K entries with full nicknames, the JSON is roughly 10_000 × ~130 bytes ≈ 1.3 MB. A relay at max capacity sends a DirectoryList that exceeds the client-side 1 MiB cap, causing the client to drop the connection with a parse error at auth time. The relay's MAX_WS_MESSAGE_BYTES also applies only to incoming messages from clients, not to outgoing messages the relay sends. Relay sends are uncapped, client receives are capped at 1 MiB. This creates a class of reachable states where a large-but-valid relay deployment permanently prevents new clients from connecting.
